name: Associated Press
credibility_notes: Gold-standard wire service, founded 1846. Nonprofit cooperative owned by member newspapers. Consistently rated among the most factual news sources worldwide. RSS via Google News filter (AP discontinued public RSS feeds)

name: Reuters
credibility_notes: Premier international wire service. Known for strict editorial standards and factual reporting. Thomson Reuters is publicly traded. RSS via Google News filter (Reuters discontinued public RSS in 2020)

name: United Press International
credibility_notes: Historic wire service founded 1907. Smaller than AP/Reuters but maintains factual reporting standards. Owned by News World Communications.

name: The New York Times
credibility_notes: Paper of record. Founded 1851. Pulitzer-winning investigative journalism. News reporting is high-quality; editorial page leans left.

name: The Washington Post
credibility_notes: Premier political newspaper. Broke Watergate. Owned by Jeff Bezos since 2013. Strong investigative and national security reporting.

name: The Wall Street Journal
credibility_notes: Leading business/financial newspaper. News reporting is highly factual; editorial page leans right. Owned by News Corp (Murdoch).

name: Los Angeles Times
credibility_notes: Largest newspaper in the Western US. Strong California and immigration coverage. Owned by Patrick Soon-Shiong.

name: Chicago Tribune
credibility_notes: Major Midwestern broadsheet, founded 1847. Historically center-right editorial page. Part of Tribune Publishing. RSS via Google News filter (Arc CMS feeds disabled)

name: The Boston Globe
credibility_notes: Leading New England newspaper. Known for Spotlight investigative team (depicted in the Oscar-winning film). Owned by John Henry.

name: CNN
credibility_notes: 24-hour cable news pioneer. Large global newsgathering operation. Opinion programming leans left; straight news is generally factual.

name: Fox News
credibility_notes: Most-watched cable news network. Strong conservative editorial voice. News desk is more factual than opinion programming. Owned by Fox Corporation.

name: MSNBC
credibility_notes: Progressive-leaning cable news. Shares newsgathering with NBC News. Primarily opinion and analysis programming. Comcast/Versant spinoff in Nov 2025.

name: NBC News
credibility_notes: Major broadcast network news division. Includes NBC Nightly News and Today. Generally factual straight news reporting.

name: CBS News
credibility_notes: Storied broadcast news division (Murrow, Cronkite legacy). Includes 60 Minutes. Generally centrist, factual reporting.

name: ABC News
credibility_notes: Major broadcast news division under Disney. World News Tonight consistently top-rated. Generally centrist reporting.

name: PBS NewsHour
credibility_notes: Publicly funded, highly respected for balanced in-depth coverage. Consistently rated among the least biased US news sources.

name: NPR
credibility_notes: Publicly funded radio network with large digital presence. High factual reporting standards. Audience skews educated/liberal but coverage aims for balance.

name: Politico
credibility_notes: Leading digital political news outlet. Strong on Beltway coverage and policy reporting. Owned by Axel Springer.

name: The Hill
credibility_notes: Congressional and policy-focused outlet. Publishes opinion from both sides. Owned by Nexstar Media Group.

name: Axios
credibility_notes: Known for concise 'Smart Brevity' format. Strong on politics and tech. Founded by Politico co-founder Jim VandeHei.

name: The Daily Beast
credibility_notes: Digital news and opinion site. Known for investigative scoops and sharp commentary. Leans left editorially. Re-classified from center-left to left based on editorial positioning.

name: Bloomberg
credibility_notes: Premier financial news service. Massive global newsgathering operation. Strong on economics, markets, and data-driven reporting. RSS via Google News filter (native feed was politics-only, missing core financial coverage)

name: CNBC
credibility_notes: Leading business news channel. Generally pro-market perspective. Factual financial reporting with market-friendly editorial stance.

name: Forbes
credibility_notes: Business magazine founded 1917. Known for rich lists and business coverage. Uses contributor model which varies in quality. Pro-market, center-right editorial slant.

name: Business Insider
credibility_notes: Large digital business and news outlet. Increasingly strong on investigative and political coverage. Owned by Axel Springer.

name: The Atlantic
credibility_notes: Prestigious magazine founded 1857. Known for long-form journalism and cultural criticism. Owned by Laurene Powell Jobs.

name: The New Yorker
credibility_notes: Elite literary and news magazine. Renowned for investigative journalism and rigorous fact-checking. Conde Nast publication. Editorial stance is clearly left-of-center; re-classified from center-left.

name: Time
credibility_notes: Iconic American news magazine founded 1923. Known for Person of the Year. Now owned by Marc Benioff.

name: The Nation
credibility_notes: Oldest continuously published weekly magazine in the US (founded 1865). Proudly progressive editorial stance. Valued for left perspective.

name: National Review
credibility_notes: Flagship conservative magazine founded by William F. Buckley Jr. in 1955. Intellectual right. Generally factual with clear conservative editorial stance.

name: Washington Examiner
credibility_notes: Conservative political publication covering Washington DC. Strong on policy and politics from a right-leaning perspective. Owned by MediaDC (Philip Anschutz).

name: The Daily Wire
credibility_notes: Conservative media company founded by Ben Shapiro. High traffic, large podcast audience. Mix of news reporting and strong opinion content. Clearly right editorial stance.

name: New York Post
credibility_notes: Oldest continuously published daily newspaper in the US (founded 1801). Owned by News Corp. Tabloid format, conservative editorial stance. Mix of tabloid sensationalism and factual news reporting.

name: Newsweek
credibility_notes: Historic American news magazine, founded 1933. Digital-first since 2012. Editorial stance has shifted toward center-to-center-right under current ownership. Factual news operation.

name: USA Today
credibility_notes: National daily with one of the largest US circulations. Gannett-owned. Known for accessible, broadly centrist coverage. Strong sports, weather, and national reporting.

name: HuffPost
credibility_notes: Large digital news outlet, founded 2005 by Arianna Huffington. Left-leaning editorial direction. Owned by HuffPost Media. Wide reach with mix of reporting and commentary.

name: Salon
credibility_notes: Online magazine founded 1995. Clearly left editorial stance on politics and culture. Mixes original reporting with strong opinion content. Long-standing digital publication.

name: Mother Jones
credibility_notes: Nonprofit investigative news organization founded 1976. Strong investigative reporting from a progressive perspective. Known for campaign finance investigations and Mitt Romney '47 percent' video.

name: Breitbart News
credibility_notes: Far-right political news and opinion website, founded 2007. High traffic and influential in right-wing media ecosystem. Strong editorial perspective; some reporting mixes news with advocacy.

name: Newsmax
credibility_notes: Conservative cable news and digital outlet. Positions itself to the right of Fox News. Large cable audience. Mix of news and strong right-wing opinion. Founded by Christopher Ruddy.

name: BBC News
credibility_notes: World's largest public broadcaster. Charter requires impartiality. Globally trusted for factual reporting, though faces criticism from both left and right in the UK.

name: The Guardian
credibility_notes: Major UK broadsheet owned by Scott Trust (nonprofit). Known for investigative journalism (Snowden leaks, Panama Papers). Open editorial stance.

name: The Independent
credibility_notes: Formerly a broadsheet, now digital-only. Centrist-to-liberal editorial line. Owned by Independent Digital News & Media; majority-owned by ADNOC (Abu Dhabi National Oil Company) since 2021 — editorial independence claimed but ownership context is relevant. Replaces The Times UK (hard paywall, no RSS).

name: The Telegraph
credibility_notes: Major UK broadsheet, founded 1855. Known as 'The Torygraph' for its conservative editorial line. Strong on UK politics and investigations.

name: Financial Times
credibility_notes: Premier global business newspaper. Known for rigorous financial and economic reporting. Owned by Nikkei. Salmon-pink pages are iconic.

name: The Economist
credibility_notes: Highly influential global affairs weekly. Self-describes as 'classical liberal.' Pro-free-market, socially liberal. Renowned for global analysis.

name: Deutsche Welle
credibility_notes: Germany's international broadcaster, funded by German federal tax revenue. Publishes in 30 languages. Known for balanced European perspective.

name: France 24
credibility_notes: French international news channel. Part of France Medias Monde (state-funded). Strong Africa and Middle East coverage.

name: El Pais (English)
credibility_notes: Spain's largest newspaper. Founded post-Franco in 1976. Strong on Latin America and European affairs. Center-left editorial line.

name: Der Spiegel (English)
credibility_notes: Germany's most influential news magazine. Known for investigative journalism. Survived a 2018 fabrication scandal with strengthened fact-checking.

name: Le Monde (English)
credibility_notes: France's newspaper of record. Founded 1944. Known for intellectual rigor and French/European political coverage. Expanding English edition.

name: ANSA (English)
credibility_notes: Italy's leading wire service, founded 1945. Cooperative owned by Italian newspapers. Primary source for Italian and Vatican news.

name: RTE News
credibility_notes: Ireland's national public broadcaster. Funded by license fee. Legally required to be impartial. Strong on Irish and EU affairs.

name: Euronews
credibility_notes: Pan-European news network broadcasting in multiple languages. Headquartered in Lyon, France. Provides European perspective on global events.

name: Al Jazeera
credibility_notes: Qatar-funded global news network. Strong Middle East and Global South coverage. Award-winning journalism, though editorial independence debated given state funding. Re-classified from 'varies' to center-left based on consistent editorial positioning.

name: Haaretz
credibility_notes: Israel's oldest daily newspaper (founded 1919). Known for critical, independent journalism. English edition widely read internationally.

name: The National
credibility_notes: Abu Dhabi-based English-language daily. Good Middle East and South Asia coverage. State-connected; generally avoids criticism of UAE government.

name: NHK World-Japan
credibility_notes: Japan's public broadcaster. Funded by viewer fees. Known for factual, restrained reporting style. Strong on Asia-Pacific news.

name: South China Morning Post
credibility_notes: Hong Kong's leading English-language newspaper, founded 1903. Owned by Alibaba. Valuable window into China/Asia, though editorial independence under scrutiny.

name: The Hindu
credibility_notes: One of India's most respected English-language dailies, founded 1878. Known for serious journalism and editorial independence. Chennai-based.

name: Channel News Asia
credibility_notes: Singapore's international news channel. Owned by Mediacorp (state-linked). Strong Asia-Pacific coverage. Generally factual though reflects Singapore's media environment.

name: Nikkei Asia
credibility_notes: English-language arm of Nikkei Inc., Japan's premier business media company. Strong on Asian business, economics, and geopolitics. Paywalled; ToS restricts redistribution — use RSS summaries only

name: The Straits Times
credibility_notes: Singapore's newspaper of record, founded 1845. Strong on Southeast Asian affairs. Operates within Singapore's press framework.

name: The Globe and Mail
credibility_notes: Canada's newspaper of record. Known for strong investigative and business reporting. Generally centrist, endorsed various parties over the years.

name: CBC News
credibility_notes: Canada's public broadcaster. Funded by parliamentary appropriation. Legally mandated to provide balanced coverage. Strong on Canadian affairs.

name: Buenos Aires Herald
credibility_notes: Relaunched English-language Argentine newspaper. Original (1876-2017) was famous for covering Dirty War disappearances. Now digital-only.

name: Mail & Guardian
credibility_notes: South Africa's leading independent newspaper. Known for anti-apartheid journalism and ongoing investigative work. Strong on African affairs.

name: The East African
credibility_notes: Regional weekly covering Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, Burundi, and South Sudan. Published by Nation Media Group, East Africa's largest media company. RSS via Google News filter (site blocks direct RSS; when:7d changed to when:24h 2026-03-19)

name: Daily Maverick
credibility_notes: South African digital-first news outlet known for investigative journalism. Its Scorpio unit has broken major corruption stories. Funded by donations and subscriptions.

name: ABC Australia
credibility_notes: Australia's national public broadcaster. Funded by government but editorially independent. Charter requires balance and accuracy.

name: Mexico News Daily
credibility_notes: English-language digital outlet covering Mexican news, politics, and culture. Founded 2016. Fills critical Latin America coverage gap. Added 2026-03-18 to address geographic gap.

name: The Brazilian Report
credibility_notes: English-language outlet covering Brazilian politics, economy, and society. Founded 2017. Subscription-based with professional editorial standards. Added 2026-03-18 to address Latin America coverage gap.

name: Yonhap News Agency
credibility_notes: South Korea's largest news agency, founded 1980. Government-affiliated but editorially independent wire service. Primary English-language source for Korean Peninsula news. Added 2026-03-18 to address South Korea coverage gap.

name: TRT World
credibility_notes: English-language arm of Turkish Radio and Television Corporation (state broadcaster). State-affiliated: editorial line generally aligns with Turkish government positions, particularly on foreign policy. Valuable for Turkish/regional perspective but readers should note state connection. Added 2026-03-18 to address Turkey coverage gap.

name: RT (Russia Today)
credibility_notes: Russian state-funded international broadcaster. Editorially reflects Russian government positions; classified as state media by multiple governments. Included for perspective on Russian/Kremlin viewpoint; editorial reliability is heavily qualified. Readers must apply strong critical lens.

name: CGTN (China Global Television Network)
credibility_notes: China's English-language international broadcaster. Part of China Media Group, state-controlled. Provides official Chinese government perspective on international affairs. Essential for understanding Beijing's messaging; editorial independence is limited. RSS via Google News filter (direct video.xml feed returned 404 as of 2026-03-19)

name: Dawn
credibility_notes: Pakistan's most widely read English-language newspaper, founded 1941. Generally independent with strong accountability journalism. Essential source for South Asian geopolitics.

name: The Times of India
credibility_notes: World's most-read English-language newspaper by circulation. Bennett, Coleman & Co. owned. Broad national coverage across Indian subcontinent. Center to center-right editorial slant.

name: The Spectator
credibility_notes: World's oldest weekly magazine (founded 1828). Conservative political and cultural commentary. Highly readable. Owned by Spectator Media. Strong conservative intellectual tradition.

name: Sky News
credibility_notes: UK commercial 24-hour news channel. Part of Sky Group (Comcast-owned). Generally balanced news reporting. Less partisan than US cable equivalents.

name: The Irish Times
credibility_notes: Ireland's newspaper of record, founded 1859. Owned by Irish Times Trust. Strong on Irish politics, European affairs, and Northern Ireland. Center-left editorial tradition.

name: The Japan Times
credibility_notes: Japan's oldest English-language newspaper, founded 1897. Majority-owned by News2u Holdings. Editorially independent with a centrist perspective on Japanese and Asian affairs.

name: ProPublica
credibility_notes: Premier nonprofit investigative newsroom. Multiple Pulitzer Prizes. Funded by donations. Investigations frequently prompt policy changes and legal action.

name: The Intercept
credibility_notes: Founded by Glenn Greenwald, Jeremy Scahill, and Laura Poitras. Strong on national security, surveillance, and civil liberties. Left-leaning editorial perspective. Re-classified to far-left based on consistent editorial positioning.

name: Bellingcat
credibility_notes: Pioneering open-source intelligence (OSINT) investigative outlet. Based in Netherlands. Known for exposing MH17 shootdown, Navalny poisoning, and war crimes.

name: Center for Public Integrity
credibility_notes: Nonprofit investigative newsroom founded 1989. Focuses on money in politics, inequality, and accountability. Multiple Pulitzer Prizes.

name: ICIJ
credibility_notes: International Consortium of Investigative Journalists. Behind Panama Papers, Pandora Papers, and other landmark cross-border investigations.

name: The Markup
credibility_notes: Nonprofit newsroom investigating technology and its impact on society. Data-driven, rigorous methodology. Founded by former ProPublica journalists.

name: The Marshall Project
credibility_notes: Nonprofit covering the US criminal justice system. Pulitzer Prize-winning. Named after Thurgood Marshall. Essential source on incarceration and policing.

name: KFF Health News
credibility_notes: Formerly Kaiser Health News. Leading nonprofit health policy newsroom. Content widely republished by major outlets. Editorially independent from KFF.

name: The Conversation
credibility_notes: Nonprofit publishing articles by academics and researchers. All articles are reviewed for accuracy. Free to republish under Creative Commons.

name: Grist
credibility_notes: Nonprofit environmental news outlet. Covers climate, energy, and environmental justice. Solutions-oriented approach. Factual science reporting.

name: Inside Climate News
credibility_notes: Pulitzer-winning nonprofit covering climate and energy. Broke the story of ExxonMobil's early climate knowledge. Factual science-based reporting.

name: Reveal (Center for Investigative Reporting)
credibility_notes: America's oldest nonprofit investigative newsroom (founded 1977). Produces documentary podcast and cross-platform investigations.

name: Reason
credibility_notes: Libertarian perspective; socially liberal, fiscally conservative. Published by Reason Foundation. Covers civil liberties, free markets, and limited government. Factual with clear libertarian perspective.

name: The American Conservative
credibility_notes: Paleoconservative/traditionalist magazine. Founded 2002 partly in opposition to Iraq War. Provides non-neoconservative right-wing perspective.

name: Jacobin
credibility_notes: Leading democratic socialist magazine. Provides explicitly left perspective on politics, economics, and culture. Important voice in American left discourse. Re-classified to far-left.

name: The American Prospect
credibility_notes: Progressive policy magazine founded 1990. Well-sourced investigative and policy journalism.

name: The Dispatch
credibility_notes: Center-right digital media company founded by Jonah Goldberg and Steve Hayes. Fact-based conservative journalism. Subscription model.

name: Lawfare
credibility_notes: Leading national security law publication. Published by Brookings. Expert legal analysis on security, intelligence, and governance issues. Migrated to Substack for distribution.

name: The Federalist
credibility_notes: Conservative online magazine covering politics, policy, and culture. Founded 2013. Provides strongly right-leaning perspective. Mix of opinion and reporting.

name: FactCheck.org
credibility_notes: Nonpartisan fact-checking project of the Annenberg Public Policy Center at the University of Pennsylvania. Checks claims from both parties.

name: Snopes
credibility_notes: Oldest and most widely known internet fact-checking site, founded 1994. Debunks rumors, urban legends, and political misinformation.

name: PolitiFact
credibility_notes: Pulitzer Prize-winning fact-checking project. Known for 'Truth-O-Meter' ratings. Operated by the Poynter Institute.

name: Premium Times Nigeria
credibility_notes: Leading Nigerian independent investigative newspaper, founded 2011. Known for anti-corruption and accountability journalism. Won multiple awards including the Wole Soyinka Award for Investigative Reporting.

name: RealClearPolitics
credibility_notes: Political news aggregator and polling data site, founded 2000. Known for poll averaging and election forecasting. Publishes opinion from across the spectrum but editorial selection leans center-right.

name: The Free Press
credibility_notes: Digital media company founded 2021 by Bari Weiss. Heterodox journalism emphasizing free speech and open debate. Center-right on cultural issues, varied on economic policy. Subscription-based with growing audience.

name: Democracy Now!
credibility_notes: Independent nonprofit media organization founded 1996 by Amy Goodman. Daily news program carried on 1,500+ stations. Explicitly progressive editorial perspective. Strong on labor, environment, peace, and social justice.

name: In These Times
credibility_notes: Socialist magazine founded 1976. Covers labor, social movements, and progressive politics from an explicitly left perspective. Founded by James Weinstein. Professional editorial staff.

name: Truthout
credibility_notes: Progressive nonprofit news outlet founded 2001. Covers social justice, environment, and civil liberties from an explicitly left perspective. Nonprofit funded by reader donations.

name: Common Dreams
credibility_notes: Progressive news and views outlet founded 1997. Nonprofit. Covers peace, environment, social justice, and inequality from a strongly progressive perspective. Mix of original reporting and aggregation.

name: Tribune (UK)
credibility_notes: British democratic socialist magazine, relaunched 2018. Historic Tribune was founded 1937 and associated with Aneurin Bevan and George Orwell. Covers UK and international politics from the left. Professional editorial operation.

name: New Statesman
credibility_notes: British centre-left political and cultural magazine, founded 1913. Historically associated with Fabian Society. Covers UK and international politics, arts, and culture. Strong investigative and analytical journalism.

name: The Progressive
credibility_notes: Progressive magazine founded 1909 by Robert M. La Follette. Covers peace, labor, civil liberties, and social justice. Long-standing publication with professional editorial team.

name: Vox
credibility_notes: Explanatory journalism outlet founded 2014 by Ezra Klein. Vox Media. Generally center-left editorial perspective with strong fact-based analysis. Good on policy and science.

name: Slate
credibility_notes: Online magazine founded 1996 by Michael Kinsley. Part of Slate Group (Univision-owned). Left-leaning editorial perspective across politics and culture. Mix of reporting and opinion.

name: The Bulwark
credibility_notes: Never-Trump conservative publication founded 2018 by Charlie Sykes and Bill Kristol. Anti-Trump right perspective. Professional journalism and analysis with conservative principles minus populist nationalism.

name: Commentary Magazine
credibility_notes: Neoconservative magazine of opinion published since 1945. Associated with American Enterprise Institute circles. Strong intellectual conservative tradition. Covers politics, culture, and foreign policy.

name: City Journal
credibility_notes: Policy and ideas magazine published by the Manhattan Institute since 1990. Focuses on urban policy, education, crime, and public administration from a conservative perspective. Rigorously edited.

name: Washington Free Beacon
credibility_notes: Conservative news website founded 2012. Known for investigative reporting on liberal institutions and Democratic politicians. Clear right-wing editorial perspective. Some original reporting with factual standards.

name: The Daily Caller
credibility_notes: Conservative news outlet co-founded by Tucker Carlson in 2010. High traffic. Right-wing editorial perspective with mix of news and opinion. Some original reporting; quality varies.

name: The Epoch Times
credibility_notes: Media outlet with ties to the Falun Gong movement. Founded 2000. Strongly anti-CCP, pro-Trump editorial perspective. Large international print and digital distribution. Credibility is limited on political topics; included for perspective representation.

name: Townhall
credibility_notes: Conservative news and opinion website, founded 1995. Originally a project of The Heritage Foundation. Now owned by Salem Communications. Mix of reporting and strong conservative opinion.

name: PJ Media
credibility_notes: Conservative opinion and news website, founded 2004. Strong voice in right-wing commentary. Owned by Salem Communications. Emphasis on politics and culture from a conservative lens.

name: The Spectator (US)
credibility_notes: American edition of the British Spectator magazine, launched 2018. Conservative political commentary and culture. Affiliated with the UK Spectator. Strong intellectual conservative tradition in American context.

name: The Washington Times
credibility_notes: Conservative broadsheet daily, founded 1982. Owned by News World Communications (Unification Church-linked). Strong on military and conservative politics. Right-leaning editorial perspective.

name: The Federalist Society Blog
credibility_notes: The Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies is the leading conservative and libertarian legal organization. Law and policy analysis from a conservative/originalist perspective. High-quality legal scholarship.

name: Mediaite
credibility_notes: Media industry news and criticism site. Founded 2009. Covers media, politics, and entertainment news. Mildly center-left editorial slant. Useful for media criticism across the spectrum.

name: openDemocracy
credibility_notes: UK-based independent global media platform covering democracy and power. Founded 2001. Left-leaning editorial perspective with international scope. Funded by foundations and donations.

name: Semafor
credibility_notes: News organization founded 2022 by Ben Smith and Justin Smith. Global ambition with offices in multiple countries. Innovative format separating news from analysis. Centrist editorial positioning.

name: The Information
credibility_notes: Subscription-based tech journalism outlet founded 2013 by Jessica Lessin. Breaking news and analysis on Silicon Valley and tech industry. High-quality reporting; centrist on politics.

name: The Wire China
credibility_notes: Digital publication covering China's economy, foreign policy, and society. Founded 2020. In-depth analysis from China experts. Centrist, analytical perspective.

name: Rest of World
credibility_notes: Nonprofit news organization covering technology's impact outside the Western tech bubble. Founded 2020. Reports from Africa, Asia, Latin America, and Middle East. Centrist, factual.

name: The Spectator (Australia)
credibility_notes: Australian edition of the Spectator. Conservative political and cultural commentary on Australian and international affairs. Founded 2008. Right-leaning editorial perspective.

name: Quillette
credibility_notes: Online magazine founded in Australia 2015 by Claire Lehmann. Heterodox intellectual perspectives, generally center-right to right on cultural issues. Covers science, culture, and politics. Strong on free speech debates.

name: UnHerd
credibility_notes: UK-based media outlet founded 2017 by Tim Montgomerie. Aims to challenge mainstream consensus from various angles. Often center-right to right editorial perspective. High-quality long-form commentary.

name: CapX
credibility_notes: Centre for Policy Studies publication. Free-market, center-right economics and politics. UK-based. High quality intellectual conservative commentary on policy and economics.

name: AEI Ideas
credibility_notes: American Enterprise Institute think-tank publication. Right-of-center economic and policy analysis. One of the most credible conservative think-tanks in the US. Expert commentary on policy, economics, and foreign affairs.

name: The Daily Signal
credibility_notes: News publication of The Heritage Foundation. Conservative policy commentary and news from one of the most influential conservative think-tanks. Clear right-wing editorial perspective with policy expertise.

name: STAT News
credibility_notes: Health, medicine, and life science news outlet. Founded 2015 by Boston Globe Media. Rigorous scientific journalism. Centrist; focused on evidence-based health reporting.

name: Science News
credibility_notes: Nonprofit science journalism organization, founded 1921. Covers all fields of science, medicine, and technology. Factual, evidence-based; politically neutral.

name: Foreign Policy
credibility_notes: Leading international affairs magazine and website. Founded 1970 by Samuel Huntington. Graham Holdings (Washington Post parent). Expert analysis on geopolitics, diplomacy, and international economics.

name: Foreign Affairs
credibility_notes: Published by the Council on Foreign Relations since 1922. Premier journal of international affairs. Expert analysis and policy discussion. Centrist, establishment foreign policy perspective.

name: War on the Rocks
credibility_notes: National security and defense analysis publication. Founded 2013. Expert commentary on military strategy, foreign policy, and security affairs. Nonpartisan, expert-driven.

name: Just Security
credibility_notes: Online forum on law, rights, and US national security. Published by NYU School of Law. Expert commentary from legal scholars and former officials. Center-left on rights issues.

name: Brookings Institution
credibility_notes: Leading centrist-to-center-left think-tank. Rigorous policy research and analysis. Covers domestic and foreign policy. Founded 1916. Expert authors from academia and government.

name: Cato at Liberty
credibility_notes: Libertarian Cato Institute policy blog. Expert analysis on economics, civil liberties, and foreign policy from a libertarian/center-right perspective. High-quality policy research.

name: The National Interest
credibility_notes: Foreign policy and national security magazine. Center-right realist perspective on international affairs. Published by the Center for the National Interest. Expert contributors from government and academia.

name: American Affairs
credibility_notes: Quarterly journal of public policy and political thought, founded 2017. Nationalist/populist right-of-center orientation. High-quality intellectual analysis of political economy and governance.

name: Prospect Magazine
credibility_notes: British center-left magazine of politics, ideas, and culture, founded 1995. In-depth analysis of UK and international politics. High-quality journalism and intellectual commentary.

name: The Wire (India)
credibility_notes: Independent Indian digital news publication, founded 2015. Known for investigative journalism and criticism of the BJP government. Center-left editorial perspective on Indian politics.

name: Daily Sabah
credibility_notes: Turkish English-language daily, founded 2014. Pro-government editorial perspective aligned with AKP/Erdogan. Important for Turkish government viewpoint on Middle East and regional affairs. Readers should note political alignment.

name: Arab News
credibility_notes: Saudi Arabia's first English-language newspaper, founded 1975. English-language coverage of Arab world affairs, international news. Saudi state-aligned editorial perspective on regional politics.

name: Middle East Eye
credibility_notes: UK-based digital news outlet covering the Middle East and North Africa. Founded 2014. Independent with center-left editorial perspective. Strong on conflict zones and human rights in the region.

name: The Africa Report
credibility_notes: Pan-African English-language news outlet, covering politics, business, and culture across the continent. Owned by Groupe Jeune Afrique (Tunis-based). Centrist analytical perspective.

name: Agence France-Presse
credibility_notes: World's third-largest wire service (after AP and Reuters). Founded 1835. Dominant global wire for Africa, Middle East, and Latin America with the deepest bureaus in those regions. French government-funded but editorially independent. Gold-standard wire journalism.

name: Politico Europe
credibility_notes: Leading English-language outlet covering EU policy, Brussels politics, and European affairs. Strong editorial standards, corrections policy, and original reporting. Essential for EU institutional coverage. Owned by Axel Springer.

name: Rappler
credibility_notes: Leading Philippine investigative news outlet. Founded by Maria Ressa (2021 Nobel Peace Prize laureate). IFCN-certified fact-checking arm. Pulitzer Prize-nominated. Known for courageous reporting on Duterte drug war. Highest-credibility SE Asian English-language source.

name: Asia Times
credibility_notes: Digital news outlet covering Asian geopolitics, finance, and culture. Successor to the original Asia Times (1995-2016). Centrist perspective on Asian affairs with expert commentary from across the region.

name: Radio Free Europe / Radio Liberty
credibility_notes: US government-funded broadcaster targeting authoritarian countries in Europe and Asia. Editorially independent and professional. Considered credible for Eastern Europe/former Soviet region coverage. Maintains journalistic standards.

name: Voice of America
credibility_notes: US federal government international broadcaster operated by USAGM. Legally prohibited from broadcasting to US audiences. Generally factual, balanced reporting though subject to US foreign policy priorities.

name: The Kyiv Independent
credibility_notes: Ukrainian English-language digital news outlet, founded 2021 after journalists left the Kyiv Post over editorial independence concerns. Primary English-language source for Ukrainian war coverage. Pro-Ukraine perspective (as expected from within Ukraine).

name: The Moscow Times
credibility_notes: Independent English-language news publication about Russia, now based in Amsterdam after fleeing Russian pressure. Founded 1992. Critical of Russian government. Important independent voice on Russia.

name: Balkan Insight (BIRN)
credibility_notes: Balkan Investigative Reporting Network. Covers Southeast European politics, war crimes, and governance. Founded 2004. Independent, factual investigative journalism in the Balkans.

name: The Diplomat
credibility_notes: Digital magazine covering geopolitics and society in the Asia-Pacific region. Founded 2002. Expert commentary on Indo-Pacific affairs, security, and diplomacy. Centrist analytical perspective.

name: Latin America Reports
credibility_notes: English-language coverage of Latin American affairs. Centrist analytical perspective on regional politics, economics, and society. Direct RSS feed confirmed live 2026-03-19 (replaced Google News when:7d proxy).

name: Religion News Service
credibility_notes: Nonprofit news agency covering religion, ethics, and spirituality since 1934. Nonpartisan. Widely syndicated in major news outlets. Essential for faith community coverage.

name: Bureau of Investigative Journalism
credibility_notes: Independent nonprofit investigative newsroom based in London. Founded 2010. Known for drone strike investigations, corporate accountability reporting. Generally center-left on editorial framing.

name: The Jerusalem Post
credibility_notes: Israel's leading English-language newspaper, founded 1932. Center-right editorial perspective on Israeli and international affairs. Strong on Middle East politics and security.

name: Tablet Magazine
credibility_notes: Jewish news and culture magazine. Founded 2009. Covers politics, culture, and ideas with a heterodox center-right perspective. Known for contrarian takes and literary journalism.

name: Jewish Telegraphic Agency
credibility_notes: Global Jewish news agency, founded 1917. Nonpartisan wire service covering Jewish communities and Israel worldwide. Widely syndicated. Essential for Jewish community coverage.

name: Dissent Magazine
credibility_notes: Democratic socialist magazine of politics and culture, founded 1954. Left-leaning intellectual analysis of politics, labor, and society. Strong analytical tradition.

name: The Post Millennial
credibility_notes: Canadian right-wing media outlet, founded 2017. Far-right editorial perspective on Canadian and international politics. High social media following. Mix of news and opinion.

name: Compact Magazine
credibility_notes: Heterodox political magazine founded 2022. Cross-ideological, but editorially right-leaning/populist nationalist in orientation. Published by prominent right-wing intellectuals. High-quality political commentary.

name: Global Times (China)
credibility_notes: Tabloid under People's Daily, official organ of the Communist Party of China. Nationalist and state-aligned editorial perspective. Nationalistic and sometimes combative in tone. Included for Chinese state/nationalist viewpoint. RSS via Google News filter (direct outboundpage.xml returned 404 as of 2026-03-19)

name: AP Fact Check
credibility_notes: Fact-checking operation of the Associated Press. Non-partisan verification of claims by politicians, public figures, and viral content. Highest factual credibility standard.

name: Persuasion
credibility_notes: Liberal-to-center-right publication founded 2020 by Yascha Mounk. Focuses on defending liberal democracy against authoritarianism from left and right. Expert contributors. Center-right on free speech and institutional issues.

name: Hindustan Times
credibility_notes: India's second-largest English-language daily, founded 1924. HT Media owned. Strong national political coverage. Broad readership across northern India. Centrist editorial line.

name: NDTV
credibility_notes: India's leading English-language news channel, founded 1988. Acquired by Adani Group in 2022; Adani's proximity to the BJP government raises editorial independence questions — editorial direction under ongoing observation post-acquisition. Strong reporting legacy. Center-left on democratic accountability.

name: The Indian Express
credibility_notes: Respected English broadsheet, founded 1932. Ramnath Goenka tradition of institutional accountability. Strong political and investigative reporting. Maintains editorial independence.

name: The Economic Times
credibility_notes: India's largest financial daily, founded 1961. Part of Times Group. Business, markets, and economic policy focus. Centrist editorial line on economic issues.

name: Business Standard
credibility_notes: India's leading independent business newspaper, founded 1975. Known for credible economic journalism. Relatively independent from political ownership. Strong on policy and markets.

name: The Print
credibility_notes: Digital news outlet founded 2017 by veteran journalist Shekhar Gupta. Strong on policy, defense, and political analysis. Editorially independent. Centrist-liberal editorial perspective.

name: India Today
credibility_notes: India's leading weekly news magazine and digital platform. TV18/India Today Group. Soft-nationalist editorial drift post-2014 but maintains journalistic standards. Broad coverage.

name: Firstpost
credibility_notes: Network18/Reliance-owned digital outlet. Editorial drift toward pro-government since 2014. Covers wide range of topics. Center-right editorial framing.

name: Deccan Herald
credibility_notes: South India-based English broadsheet (Bengaluru), founded 1948. Covers Karnataka and South Indian politics. Independent ownership by The Printers (Mysore) Group.

name: LiveMint
credibility_notes: English-language business newspaper by HT Media, launched 2007 in association with Wall Street Journal. Strong on economic policy, markets, and corporate news. Centrist editorial line.

name: The Telegraph India
credibility_notes: Kolkata-based English broadsheet, founded 1982 by ABP Group. Strong on eastern India and national politics. Known for editorial independence and critical coverage of government. RSS via Google News.

name: Tribune India
credibility_notes: Chandigarh-based English daily, founded 1881 in Lahore. Trust-owned (no corporate proprietor). Strong on northern India, Punjab, Haryana politics. Centrist editorial line. RSS via Google News.

name: The Pioneer
credibility_notes: Delhi-based English daily, founded 1865. One of India's oldest newspapers. Editorially leans center-right, sympathetic to BJP/NDA positions. RSS via Google News.

name: News18
credibility_notes: Network18/Reliance-owned TV news and digital platform. Broad reach across India. Soft-nationalist editorial drift post-Reliance acquisition. Center-right framing on national issues.

name: Outlook India
credibility_notes: Indian news magazine, founded 1995. Long-form journalism and political analysis. Changed ownership in 2023 (Indigo Brands), editorial direction under observation. Historically center-left. RSS via Google News.

name: The News Minute
credibility_notes: South India-focused digital outlet, founded 2014. Covers Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh. Strong on regional politics, gender issues, and accountability reporting.

name: Zee News
credibility_notes: Zee Media Corporation TV channel, founded 1992. Hindi-first but English digital presence. Nationalist editorial framing, pro-government lean. Provides right-wing voice in India's TV news landscape.

name: Press Trust of India
credibility_notes: India's largest news agency, founded 1947. Cooperative owned by Indian newspapers. Factual wire service providing baseline news coverage across India. RSS via Google News.

name: Asian News International
credibility_notes: India's largest private news agency, founded 1971. Major video wire service. Criticized for uncritical relay of government press releases. Center-right lean reflects source access patterns. RSS via Google News.

name: Scroll.in
credibility_notes: Independent Indian digital outlet founded 2014. Known for long-form journalism, investigative reports, and arts coverage. Donor/reader-funded model. Center-left secular-liberal perspective.

name: The Quint
credibility_notes: Digital outlet with strong video-first approach. Has a fact-checking arm (QuintFit). Bloomberg-backed. Center-left on democratic accountability and human rights.

name: Newslaundry
credibility_notes: Subscription-funded independent media outlet founded 2012. Covers media accountability and original investigative reporting. Full transparency on funding. Center-left perspective.

name: Alt News
credibility_notes: India's premier independent fact-checking organization, founded 2017. IFCN signatory. Transparent methodology. Essential counterweight to political misinformation.

name: The Caravan
credibility_notes: Long-form journalism magazine. Investigative and literary. Critical of the Modi government. Widely respected in Indian media circles. Founded 1988, relaunched 2010.

name: Swarajya
credibility_notes: Online magazine representing the Hindu nationalist intellectual right, founded 2014. Pro-BJP, pro-Hindu civilizational perspective. Generally factual but with a strong right-wing editorial framework.

name: Republic TV
credibility_notes: Founded by Arnab Goswami. Aggressively pro-BJP, high ratings. Represents a genuine right-of-center viewpoint in Indian media. Contested editorial standards — credibility caution noted.

name: BOOM
credibility_notes: Independent fact-checking organization, founded 2014. IFCN signatory. Fact-checks claims across the Indian political spectrum. Transparent methodology, reader-funded. Native RSS stale since 2022; Google News fallback.

name: Greater Kashmir
credibility_notes: Kashmir's largest English daily, founded 1989. Covers Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh. Essential regional voice on India's most sensitive conflict zone. Native RSS feed. Center-left on civil liberties and autonomy issues.

name: The Kathmandu Post
credibility_notes: Nepal's leading English-language daily, founded 1993. Published by Kantipur Publications (Nepal's largest media group). Strong on Nepali politics and governance.

name: Nepali Times
credibility_notes: Nepal's leading English-language weekly magazine, founded 2000. Published by Himalmedia. Known for in-depth analysis of South Asian politics, environment, and culture.

name: Dhaka Tribune
credibility_notes: Bangladesh's prominent English-language daily, founded 2013. Independent editorial stance. Covers Bangladeshi politics, economy, and South Asian affairs. RSS via Google News.

name: Daily Mirror Sri Lanka
credibility_notes: Sri Lanka's leading English-language daily, published by Wijeya Newspapers. Covers Sri Lankan politics, economy, and South Asian affairs. Centrist editorial line. RSS via Google News.

name: National Post
credibility_notes: Canada's center-right newspaper of record. Part of Postmedia Network. Founded 1998 by Conrad Black. MBFC: Right-Center, High Credibility. Strong on Canadian federal politics and business.

name: Toronto Star
credibility_notes: Canada's largest-circulation daily newspaper. Founded 1892. MBFC: Left-Center, High Credibility. Strong investigative tradition.

name: The Guardian Nigeria
credibility_notes: Independent, credible Nigerian broadsheet. Founded 1983. MBFC: Center, High Credibility. Essential for West African coverage.

name: Focus Taiwan (CNA)
credibility_notes: Taiwan's Central News Agency English service. Government-funded but editorially credible. MBFC: Left-Center, High Factual. Essential for Taiwan Strait coverage. RSS switched from deprecated FeedBurner to native feed.

name: The Jakarta Post
credibility_notes: Indonesia's leading English-language daily. Founded 1983. Independent editorial identity. Indonesia is the 4th most populous nation with zero prior coverage.

name: Bangkok Post
credibility_notes: Thailand's oldest English-language daily. Founded 1946. Newspaper of record. Comprehensive Thai and regional SE Asian coverage.

name: Al-Monitor
credibility_notes: Leading English-language Middle East independent. MBFC: Left-Center, High Credibility. Covers Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, Syria gaps with professional journalists and Persian-language analysts.

name: Korea Herald
credibility_notes: South Korea's largest English-language daily. Founded 1953. Sole South Korean member of Asia News Network. Newspaper perspective alongside Yonhap wire.

name: Times of Israel
credibility_notes: Centrist English-language Israeli digital outlet. Large international readership. Founded 2012. Fills center gap between Haaretz (left) and Jerusalem Post (right).

name: Africanews
credibility_notes: Pan-African news channel. Euronews sister channel. 40M household reach across 35 African countries. Editorial charter guarantees independence.

name: Nation Africa
credibility_notes: Kenya's largest media group. Oldest English-language daily in East Africa. Nation Media Group. Covers Kenya and broader African affairs.

name: RNZ News
credibility_notes: New Zealand's public broadcaster. Government-funded, editorially independent. Strong Pacific and NZ coverage. New Zealand and Pacific Islands were entirely absent from previous list.

name: SBS News
credibility_notes: Australia's multicultural public broadcaster. Strong on Asia-Pacific, immigration, and global communities. Different demographic perspective from ABC Australia.

name: The Daily Star
credibility_notes: Bangladesh's most widely read English-language daily. MBFC: Center, High Credibility. Bangladesh (170M people) had zero coverage. Covers garment industry, Rohingya crisis, climate displacement.

name: Africa Check
credibility_notes: Africa's first independent fact-checking organization. MBFC: Least Biased, Very High Factual. IFCN certified. Offices in South Africa, Kenya, Nigeria, Senegal.

name: Yle News
credibility_notes: Finland's public broadcaster English service. Finland post-NATO accession is geopolitically vital. Strong Arctic, Russia border, and Nordic coverage.

name: Eurasianet
credibility_notes: Independent nonprofit covering Central Asia and Caucasus. MBFC: Center, High Credibility. Only credible English-language source on Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, and the post-Soviet space. Fills complete Central Asia void.

name: Notes from Poland
credibility_notes: English-language service covering Polish politics and rule-of-law issues. Poland is the EU's most contested democracy; central test case for democratic backsliding.

name: MercoPress
credibility_notes: South Atlantic News Agency. Founded 1990. Covers Argentina, Chile, Uruguay, Paraguay, Bolivia in English. Fills Southern Cone Latin America gap.

name: Handelsblatt Global
credibility_notes: Germany's leading business daily. English edition focuses on EU economics and German industrial policy. Fills business/economic gap DW and Der Spiegel don't cover.

name: Malay Mail
credibility_notes: Independent Malaysian online newspaper. MBFC: Center. Adds Malaysia (33M people) to SE Asian coverage.

name: CTV News
credibility_notes: Canada's most-watched TV news network. Bell Media. Centrist editorial positioning.

name: Radio Free Asia
credibility_notes: US government-funded broadcaster covering China, North Korea, SE Asia from free-press angle. Fills BenarNews void (shut down May 2025). Editorially independent despite government funding.

name: The Standard
credibility_notes: Kenya's second-largest newspaper. The Standard Group. Alternative perspective alongside Nation Africa for East African coverage.

name: Ghanaian Times
credibility_notes: State-owned daily, founded 1958. Ghana's newspaper of record.

name: MyJoyOnline
credibility_notes: Ghana's leading independent digital news platform. Commercial ownership.

name: The Nation Nigeria
credibility_notes: Nigeria's second-largest broadsheet, founded 2006.

name: Vanguard Nigeria
credibility_notes: One of Nigeria's oldest dailies, founded 1984. Independent ownership.

name: Punch Nigeria
credibility_notes: Nigeria's most-read newspaper. Founded 1971. Strong investigative tradition.

name: Daily Graphic
credibility_notes: Ghana's highest-circulation daily. State-owned but editorially credible.

name: FrontPage Africa
credibility_notes: Liberia's leading independent investigative newspaper. Founded 2003.

name: Addis Standard
credibility_notes: Ethiopia's leading independent English-language magazine. Essential for Horn of Africa.

name: Daily Monitor
credibility_notes: Uganda's leading independent newspaper, founded 1992. Nation Media Group.

name: The Observer Uganda
credibility_notes: Uganda's leading independent investigative outlet. Founded 2004.

name: The Citizen Tanzania
credibility_notes: Tanzania's leading independent English daily.

name: The New Times Rwanda
credibility_notes: Rwanda's only English-language daily. Government-affiliated.

name: NewsDay Zimbabwe
credibility_notes: Zimbabwe's leading independent daily. Alpha Media Holdings.

name: The Zimbabwe Independent
credibility_notes: Zimbabwe's leading independent weekly. Critical of Mnangagwa government.

name: The Namibian
credibility_notes: Namibia's leading independent newspaper, founded 1985 during apartheid occupation.

name: The New Humanitarian
credibility_notes: Formerly IRIN News. Independent nonprofit covering humanitarian crises globally. Primary source for DRC, CAR, Sudan, Yemen.

name: The Conversation Africa
credibility_notes: African arm of The Conversation. Academic-to-public analysis by African researchers.

name: L'Orient Today
credibility_notes: Lebanon's English-language daily. Arm of historic L'Orient-Le Jour (1971).

name: Jordan Times
credibility_notes: Jordan's leading English-language daily, founded 1975.

name: Gulf News
credibility_notes: UAE's most widely read English-language newspaper. Al Nisr Publishing. Founded 1978.

name: Morocco World News
credibility_notes: Morocco's leading English-language digital outlet. Founded 2011.

name: The New Arab
credibility_notes: English outlet of Al-Araby Al-Jadeed. Qatari-funded, independent editorial.

name: Kurdistan 24
credibility_notes: English-language outlet for Kurdistan Region of Iraq and Kurdish affairs.

name: Iran International
credibility_notes: UK-based English section. Primary source for Iran opposition and society.

name: Egypt Independent
credibility_notes: English arm of Al-Masry Al-Youm, Egypt's largest independent newspaper.

name: The Media Line
credibility_notes: Middle East news agency. Nonpartisan editorial mandate. Founded 1992.

name: Middle East Monitor
credibility_notes: UK-based nonprofit. Pro-Palestinian perspective. Balance to pro-Israel sources.

name: Asharq Al-Awsat
credibility_notes: Pan-Arab daily, London-published. Saudi-funded (MBC Group). Gulf establishment perspective.

name: Nawaat
credibility_notes: Tunisia's leading independent investigative platform. Founded 2004. Key Arab Spring source.

name: The News International
credibility_notes: Pakistan's largest English-language daily. Jang Group.

name: The Express Tribune
credibility_notes: Pakistan's second English-language daily. NYT partnership.

name: TOLOnews
credibility_notes: Afghanistan's leading independent TV/digital news. Maintained operations through Taliban takeover.

name: Pajhwok Afghan News
credibility_notes: Afghanistan's leading independent news agency. Operates under Taliban with restricted reporting.

name: The Astana Times
credibility_notes: Kazakhstan's English-language newspaper. Government-affiliated.

name: Caixin Global
credibility_notes: China's leading financial news company. More editorially independent than state media.

name: The Irrawaddy
credibility_notes: Myanmar's most respected independent news magazine. Thailand-based. Primary coup coverage.

name: Myanmar Now
credibility_notes: Independent Myanmar news outlet covering post-coup crisis.

name: Phnom Penh Post
credibility_notes: Cambodia's oldest English-language newspaper (1992).

name: VnExpress International
credibility_notes: English arm of Vietnam's largest news website.

name: Malaysiakini
credibility_notes: Malaysia's leading independent news portal. Subscription-funded.

name: Hong Kong Free Press
credibility_notes: HK's leading independent outlet. Crowdfunded. Essential post-National Security Law.

name: Taipei Times
credibility_notes: Taiwan's leading English daily. Liberty Times Group. Pro-democracy.

name: China Media Project
credibility_notes: Expert analysis of Chinese media and propaganda. University of Hong Kong.

name: The Third Pole
credibility_notes: Nonprofit covering water and environment in Hindu Kush Himalaya region.

name: RNZ Pacific
credibility_notes: Radio New Zealand's Pacific service. Primary source for Pacific Islands news.

name: ERR News
credibility_notes: Estonian public broadcaster. Primary English source for Baltic affairs and NATO's eastern flank.

name: LSM.lv
credibility_notes: Latvia's public broadcaster English service.

name: LRT English
credibility_notes: Lithuanian public broadcaster English service.

name: Hungarian Free Press
credibility_notes: English-language independent outlet covering Hungary. Critical of Orban government.

name: Radio Prague International
credibility_notes: Czech Radio's international English service. Since 1936.

name: Kafkadesk
credibility_notes: Paris-based covering Central and Eastern European affairs.

name: Emerging Europe
credibility_notes: Business-focused outlet covering 23 Central and Eastern European countries.

name: N1 Info
credibility_notes: Regional news channel covering Serbia, Bosnia, Croatia. CNN affiliate.

name: OCCRP
credibility_notes: Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting Project. Network of 40+ investigative centers.

name: Georgia Today
credibility_notes: English-language Georgian newspaper. South Caucasus affairs.

name: JAMnews
credibility_notes: Independent news agency covering South Caucasus (Georgia, Azerbaijan, Armenia).

name: SWI Swissinfo.ch
credibility_notes: Swiss Broadcasting Corporation's international service. Covers UN, WTO, Red Cross in Geneva.

name: DutchNews.nl
credibility_notes: The Netherlands' leading English-language news site.

name: Euractiv
credibility_notes: EU policy media network. Essential for EU legislative process. Brussels-based.

name: Caracas Chronicles
credibility_notes: Independent outlet covering Venezuelan politics under Maduro.

name: Colombia Reports
credibility_notes: Colombia's leading English-language online news source.

name: Peru Reports
credibility_notes: English-language coverage of Peruvian politics.

name: The Tico Times
credibility_notes: Costa Rica's English-language newspaper, founded 1956.

name: El Faro
credibility_notes: El Salvador's leading investigative outlet. Award-winning Bukele and gang coverage.

name: InSight Crime
credibility_notes: Leading independent organization studying organized crime in Latin America.

name: Jamaica Gleaner
credibility_notes: Jamaica's newspaper of record, founded 1834. Oldest Anglophone Caribbean paper.

name: Trinidad Express
credibility_notes: Trinidad and Tobago's most-read newspaper.

name: The Tyee
credibility_notes: British Columbia's independent investigative outlet. Reader-funded.

name: New Zealand Herald
credibility_notes: New Zealand's highest-circulation newspaper. Founded 1863.

name: The Guardian Australia
credibility_notes: Australian arm of The Guardian. Scott Trust ownership.

name: Crikey
credibility_notes: Australia's leading independent digital news outlet. Subscriber-funded.

name: America Magazine
credibility_notes: Jesuit magazine. Founded 1909. Progressive Catholic intellectual perspective.

name: National Catholic Reporter
credibility_notes: Independent Catholic journalism. Founded 1964. Broke clerical abuse stories.

name: Christianity Today
credibility_notes: Flagship evangelical magazine. Founded 1956 by Billy Graham.

name: The Christian Post
credibility_notes: Leading evangelical news site. Conservative evangelical perspective.

name: The Tablet
credibility_notes: Britain's leading Catholic weekly. Founded 1840. Liberal Catholic tradition.

name: The Forward
credibility_notes: Jewish American news org. Founded 1897. Center-left on Jewish affairs and Israel.

name: Crux
credibility_notes: Independent Catholic news. Most authoritative English Vatican source.

name: Catholic Herald
credibility_notes: UK Catholic weekly. Traditionalist-to-orthodox perspective. Founded 1888.

name: Sojourners
credibility_notes: Progressive evangelical magazine. Social justice and peace. Founded 1971.

name: Carbon Brief
credibility_notes: Gold standard for climate journalism accuracy. Data-driven. Founded 2011.

name: Climate Home News
credibility_notes: Covers climate policy and international negotiations.

name: SciDev.Net
credibility_notes: Science and development news for the Global South. Wellcome Trust-funded.

name: MIT Technology Review
credibility_notes: World's longest-running tech publication. Founded 1899. AI, biotech, climate tech.

name: Ars Technica
credibility_notes: In-depth technology and science journalism. Conde Nast.

name: The Register
credibility_notes: UK tech journalism. Enterprise tech, AI policy, cybersecurity.

name: Nature News
credibility_notes: World's leading scientific journal. Springer Nature. Founded 1869.

name: Science Magazine
credibility_notes: AAAS flagship. Second to Nature in prestige.

name: Wired
credibility_notes: Influential tech and culture magazine. Founded 1993. Conde Nast.

name: New Scientist
credibility_notes: Weekly science and technology news. Founded 1956.

name: The Conversation
credibility_notes: Global academic-to-public analysis. Founded 2011.

name: IEEE Spectrum
credibility_notes: IEEE's technology magazine. Engineering and technology.

name: Defense One
credibility_notes: Leading independent defense and national security outlet.

name: The War Zone
credibility_notes: In-depth military and defense technology journalism. FOIA-based.

name: Breaking Defense
credibility_notes: Leading defense industry and policy news. Pentagon acquisition.

name: RUSI Commentary
credibility_notes: Royal United Services Institute. UK's premier defense think-tank. Founded 1831.

name: C4ISRNET
credibility_notes: Military technology outlet. Command, control, intelligence systems.

name: Council on Foreign Relations
credibility_notes: Premier US foreign policy think-tank. Publisher of Foreign Affairs.

name: Chatham House
credibility_notes: Royal Institute of International Affairs. Premier UK think-tank. Founded 1920.

name: CSIS
credibility_notes: Center for Strategic and International Studies. Bipartisan. Founded 1962.

name: Atlantic Council
credibility_notes: Pro-transatlantic think-tank. NATO, Europe, global security. Founded 1961.

name: ECFR
credibility_notes: European Council on Foreign Relations. EU strategy. 9 EU offices.

name: Lowy Institute
credibility_notes: Australia's leading foreign policy think-tank. Indo-Pacific focus.

name: ISEAS Yusof Ishak Institute
credibility_notes: Singapore's premier SE Asian studies institute. ASEAN focus.

name: Observer Research Foundation
credibility_notes: India's premier private foreign policy think-tank. Indo-Pacific strategy.

name: Wilson Center
credibility_notes: Congressionally-chartered. Bipartisan international affairs. Founded 1968.

name: Devex
credibility_notes: Global development news. Foreign aid, UN, World Bank. Founded 2000.

name: The Root
credibility_notes: Black American news and politics. Founded 2008 by Skip Gates.

name: ICT News
credibility_notes: Indigenous American news. Founded 1981. Covers 574 federally recognized tribes.

name: Latino Rebels
credibility_notes: Independent Latino political commentary. Immigration, Puerto Rico.

name: Global Voices
credibility_notes: International citizen journalism. 167 countries. Founded 2004 at Harvard. CC licensed.

name: Jurist
credibility_notes: Legal news by law faculty and practitioners. University of Pittsburgh.

name: Investigate Europe
credibility_notes: Cross-border European investigative journalism. Nonprofit.

name: Forbidden Stories
credibility_notes: Continues reporting of killed/jailed journalists. Behind Pegasus Project.

name: Correctiv
credibility_notes: Germany's leading nonprofit investigative newsroom.

name: +972 Magazine
credibility_notes: Independent Israeli-Palestinian media. Covers occupation from both perspectives.

name: The Lever
credibility_notes: Nonprofit investigative newsroom. Corporate influence on US politics.

name: Lighthouse Reports
credibility_notes: Amsterdam-based cross-border investigative. Migration and border policy.

name: Tortoise Media
credibility_notes: UK slow journalism. Founded 2019 by James Harding (ex-BBC). Member-funded.

name: African Business
credibility_notes: Premier pan-African business magazine. IC Publications. Founded 1966.

name: Follow The Money
credibility_notes: Nigeria-based data journalism. Tracks public spending. Award-winning.

name: Global News
credibility_notes: Corus Entertainment. Canada's second national commercial broadcast network.

name: The Hill Times
credibility_notes: Ottawa-based political coverage. Covers Parliament Hill and federal policy.

name: i News
credibility_notes: Independent-leaning UK digital newspaper. Owned by Daily Mail group but maintains editorial independence.

name: iPolitics
credibility_notes: Subscriber-funded parliamentary journalism. Essential for federal politics coverage.

name: Maclean's
credibility_notes: Canada's weekly magazine of record. Est. 1905.

name: Montreal Gazette
credibility_notes: Postmedia. Quebec English-language daily. Essential for bilingual Canada coverage.

name: National Observer
credibility_notes: Investigative journalism focused on climate and politics. Est. 2014.

name: Ricochet Media
credibility_notes: Left investigative journalism. English and French editions. Balances right-leaning outlets.

name: The Conversation UK
credibility_notes: Academic-to-public journalism. Expert-written, peer-reviewed. Strong factual rigor.

name: True North
credibility_notes: Right-leaning Canadian digital media. Provides conservative voice in Canadian landscape.

name: OpIndia
credibility_notes: Pro-BJP digital media. Strong right-wing/Hindu nationalist editorial line. Frequently fact-checked for misleading claims. Included for far-right spectrum coverage in India edition.

name: Organiser
credibility_notes: Weekly magazine affiliated with RSS (Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh). Oldest running publication of Hindu nationalist movement, founded 1947. Included for far-right spectrum coverage.

name: People's Democracy
credibility_notes: Official organ of the Communist Party of India (Marxist). Marxist-Leninist perspective on Indian politics. Included for far-left spectrum coverage.

name: WION
credibility_notes: Zee Media international news channel. India-first editorial perspective. Nationalist framing on geopolitical issues. Part of Essel Group.

name: La Jornada
credibility_notes: Major Mexican daily with left-wing editorial stance. Founded 1984. Supports labor and indigenous rights causes. Spanish-language (RSS via Google News).

name: Morning Star
credibility_notes: British socialist daily newspaper. Cooperative-owned. Supports trade union and labour movement. Founded 1930. Only English-language socialist daily.

name: Al Arabiya English
credibility_notes: Saudi-owned pan-Arab news channel. Counterpart to Al Jazeera from Gulf perspective. Pro-Saudi editorial line on regional issues.

name: i24NEWS
credibility_notes: Israeli international news channel. Broadcasts in English, French, Arabic. Center-right on Israeli/regional issues.

name: La Nacion
credibility_notes: Major Argentine daily, founded 1870. Conservative/liberal editorial stance. One of Latin America's most influential newspapers.

name: The Australian
credibility_notes: Australia's only national broadsheet. Owned by News Corp (Murdoch). Center-right editorial stance with strong business and political coverage.

name: Spiked
credibility_notes: British online magazine promoting free speech and libertarian perspectives. Originally evolved from Living Marxism but shifted to right-libertarian stance.

name: The Blaze
credibility_notes: Conservative media company founded by Glenn Beck. Mix of news, opinion, and commentary. AllSides: Right.

name: The American Spectator
credibility_notes: Conservative monthly magazine founded 1967. Known for investigative journalism from a right-leaning perspective.

name: Just The News
credibility_notes: Founded by John Solomon (former Washington Times, The Hill). Focuses on government accountability from conservative perspective.

name: The Western Journal
credibility_notes: Conservative news and commentary. Founded 2008 by Floyd Brown. AllSides: Right. MBFC: Right.

name: Rebel News
credibility_notes: Canadian far-right online media company. Founded by Ezra Levant. Included for spectrum completeness in Canadian coverage.

name: The Daily Telegraph (AU)
credibility_notes: Sydney-based tabloid owned by News Corp Australia. Center-right editorial stance. Australia's highest-circulation daily.

name: The Philadelphia Inquirer
credibility_notes: Philadelphia's newspaper of record, founded 1829. Pulitzer Prize-winning investigative journalism. Strong political and national coverage.

name: Miami Herald
credibility_notes: South Florida's newspaper of record. Strong on Latin America, immigration, and Florida politics. McClatchy/Chatham Asset Management.

name: Houston Chronicle
credibility_notes: Texas's largest newspaper, founded 1901. Essential for energy sector, NASA, and Gulf Coast coverage. Hearst-owned.

name: Star Tribune
credibility_notes: Minneapolis-St. Paul's newspaper of record. Largest in the Upper Midwest. Strong on agriculture, climate, and social policy.

name: San Francisco Chronicle
credibility_notes: Northern California's dominant newspaper, founded 1865. Strong on tech industry, housing, and West Coast politics. Hearst-owned.

name: The Dallas Morning News
credibility_notes: Dallas-Fort Worth's newspaper of record, founded 1885. Nine Pulitzer Prizes. Strong on Texas politics and Southwest affairs.

name: The Seattle Times
credibility_notes: Pacific Northwest's largest newspaper, founded 1891. Family-owned (Blethen). Strong on tech, Boeing, environment, and Northwest politics.

name: The Atlanta Journal-Constitution
credibility_notes: Southeast's dominant newspaper, founded 1868. Pulitzer Prize-winning. Essential for Southern politics, civil rights, and CDC coverage. Cox Enterprises.

name: The Denver Post
credibility_notes: Colorado's largest newspaper, founded 1892. Four Pulitzer Prizes. Strong on Mountain West politics, public lands, and water issues. RSS via Google News (native feed blocked by bot protection).

name: Tampa Bay Times
credibility_notes: Florida's largest newspaper, founded 1884. Home to PolitiFact. 13 Pulitzer Prizes. Strong investigative tradition. Nonprofit Poynter Institute-owned.

name: Daily Mail
credibility_notes: World's most-visited English-language news website. Founded 1896. Right-leaning editorial stance. Tabloid format but covers every major international story. DMGT (Rothermere).

name: The Sun
credibility_notes: UK's highest-circulation tabloid. Founded 1964. Right-leaning editorial. News Corp (Murdoch). Covers all major national and international stories.

name: TechCrunch
credibility_notes: Leading tech industry news outlet, founded 2005. Covers startups, AI, venture capital, and Silicon Valley. Yahoo/Apollo Global Management.

name: The Verge
credibility_notes: Technology and culture news outlet, founded 2011. Strong on consumer tech, AI policy, and digital rights. Vox Media.

name: MarketWatch
credibility_notes: Financial news and data. Dow Jones (News Corp). Founded 1997. Essential for markets, economic data, and business coverage.

name: Fox Business
credibility_notes: Business news network, Fox Corporation. Center-right perspective on economics and markets. More policy-focused than Fox News. Covers major economic stories.

name: New York Sun
credibility_notes: Relaunched 2022 as digital broadsheet. Center-right editorial perspective. Quality conservative journalism with editorial standards. Founded originally 2002.

name: Australian Financial Review
credibility_notes: Australia's premier financial daily. Nine Entertainment. Pro-market, center-right editorial stance. Essential for Australian business and economic policy.

name: Kyodo News
credibility_notes: Japan's largest news agency, founded 1945. Nonprofit cooperative owned by 52 Japanese media organizations. Covers Asia-Pacific comprehensively. English service active.

name: Anadolu Agency
credibility_notes: Turkey's state-run wire service, founded 1920. Covers Middle East, Balkans, Central Asia. State-affiliated; reflects Turkish government positions on foreign policy. English service available.

name: Stars and Stripes
credibility_notes: Congressionally funded US military newspaper, founded 1861. Editorially independent from DoD. Essential for military personnel, veterans, and defense coverage. RSS via Google News (native feeds defunct).

name: Defense News
credibility_notes: Leading defense industry and military policy publication. Sightline Media Group. Covers Pentagon procurement, global defense, military technology.

name: SCOTUSblog
credibility_notes: Authoritative US Supreme Court coverage, founded 2002. Run by practicing Supreme Court lawyers. Only news outlet to have argued before SCOTUS. Nonpartisan legal analysis.

name: The Sydney Morning Herald
credibility_notes: Australia's oldest newspaper, founded 1831. Sydney-based. Nine Entertainment. Strong on Australian politics and Asia-Pacific affairs.

name: Hürriyet Daily News
credibility_notes: Turkey's oldest English-language newspaper, founded 1961. Provides centrist perspective alongside TRT World (state) and Daily Sabah (pro-government). Demirören Holding. RSS via Google News (native feed unreliable).

name: Sky News Australia
credibility_notes: Australian 24-hour news channel. News Corp Australia. Right-leaning editorial stance. Provides conservative Australian perspective on national and international affairs.

name: DPA International
credibility_notes: Germany's national news agency, founded 1949. Fourth-largest wire service globally. Strong on EU/NATO.

name: EFE (English)
credibility_notes: Spain's national wire service. Dominant in Latin America. Founded 1939.

name: IANS
credibility_notes: India's second-largest news agency after PTI. Founded 1986.

name: PAP (Polish Press Agency)
credibility_notes: Poland's national wire service, founded 1918.

name: Bernama
credibility_notes: Malaysian National News Agency, founded 1968.

name: Full Fact
credibility_notes: UK's independent fact-checking charity. IFCN signatory. Founded 2010.

name: AFP Fact Check
credibility_notes: AFP's global fact-checking arm. IFCN signatory. 80+ countries, 25 languages.

name: Logically
credibility_notes: AI-assisted fact-checking platform. IFCN-certified. Founded 2017.

name: VERA Files
credibility_notes: Philippine independent fact-checking. IFCN signatory.

name: GB News
credibility_notes: UK conservative news channel, launched 2021. Growing audience. Right editorial stance.

name: Daily Express
credibility_notes: UK right-leaning tabloid, founded 1900. Reach Media group.

name: The Times
credibility_notes: Britain's newspaper of record, founded 1785. News UK (Murdoch). Center-right. RSS via Google News (hard paywall).

name: PoliticsHome
credibility_notes: UK political news covering Westminster and Parliament.

name: The Canary
credibility_notes: UK left-wing digital media, founded 2015. Reader-funded.

name: Novara Media
credibility_notes: UK left-wing digital media, founded 2011. Strong on labor, housing, climate.

name: Byline Times
credibility_notes: UK investigative outlet, founded 2018. Reader-funded. Accountability journalism.

name: The Herald (Scotland)
credibility_notes: Scotland's oldest national newspaper, founded 1783.

name: Belfast Telegraph
credibility_notes: Northern Ireland's most-read newspaper. Essential for NI politics and post-Brexit border.

name: The National (Scotland)
credibility_notes: Scotland's pro-independence daily. Founded 2014.

name: EUobserver
credibility_notes: Brussels-based independent EU affairs outlet. Nonprofit. Founded 2000.

name: Kathimerini English
credibility_notes: Greece's newspaper of record, English edition. Founded 1919.

name: Romania Insider
credibility_notes: Romania's leading English-language outlet. Founded 2009.

name: The Sofia Globe
credibility_notes: Bulgaria's English-language news outlet.

name: Croatia Week
credibility_notes: Croatia's English-language news outlet.

name: The Brussels Times
credibility_notes: English-language daily covering Belgium and EU affairs. Founded 2019.

name: The Local Germany
credibility_notes: English-language news covering Germany. EU's largest economy.

name: The Local France
credibility_notes: English-language news covering France. P5 power and EU co-leader.

name: The Local Spain
credibility_notes: English-language news covering Spain. EU's fourth-largest economy.

name: Detroit Free Press
credibility_notes: Michigan's largest newspaper, founded 1831. Gannett. Auto industry, Midwest politics.

name: The Arizona Republic
credibility_notes: Arizona's largest newspaper, founded 1890. Key swing state. Strong on immigration, border, water.

name: The Oregonian
credibility_notes: Oregon's largest newspaper, founded 1850. Advance Publications.

name: St. Louis Post-Dispatch
credibility_notes: Missouri's largest newspaper, founded 1878. Lee Enterprises. Ferguson/racial justice coverage.

name: Deseret News
credibility_notes: Salt Lake City newspaper, founded 1850. LDS Church ownership. Center-right editorial. Religion, family policy, Western US.

name: The 19th*
credibility_notes: Nonprofit covering gender, politics, and policy. Founded 2020.

name: Courthouse News Service
credibility_notes: National legal news service covering federal and state courts. Founded 1990.

name: High Country News
credibility_notes: Nonprofit covering the American West — public lands, water, Indigenous affairs. Founded 1970.

name: Jewish News Syndicate
credibility_notes: Jewish news wire service. Center-right on Israel/Jewish affairs.

name: The Texan
credibility_notes: Texas-focused digital news, founded 2018. Center-right. State legislature, energy, border, education.

name: Pittsburgh Post-Gazette
credibility_notes: Pittsburgh's newspaper of record, founded 1786. Shifted center-right. Key Rust Belt perspective.

name: The Sacramento Bee
credibility_notes: California state capital newspaper, founded 1857. McClatchy.

name: Las Vegas Review-Journal
credibility_notes: Nevada's largest newspaper, founded 1909. Adelson family. Center-right. Key swing state.

name: The Charlotte Observer
credibility_notes: North Carolina's largest newspaper, founded 1886. McClatchy. Swing state, US #2 financial center.

name: The Salt Lake Tribune
credibility_notes: Utah's independent newspaper. First major US paper to go nonprofit (2019). Pulitzer-winning.

name: CNSNews
credibility_notes: Conservative news outlet. Media Research Center. AllSides: Right.

name: Western Standard
credibility_notes: Western Canadian right-leaning news. Alberta/prairies conservative perspective.

name: News24
credibility_notes: South Africa's largest digital news platform. Media24/Naspers. Founded 1998.

name: TheCable
credibility_notes: Nigerian digital news and investigative outlet. Founded 2014.

name: Tehran Times
credibility_notes: Iran's English-language daily. State-affiliated. Iranian government perspective.

name: The Peninsula Qatar
credibility_notes: Qatar's English-language daily.

name: Manila Bulletin
credibility_notes: Philippines' oldest broadsheet, founded 1900. Complements Rappler.

name: The Nation Thailand
credibility_notes: Thai English-language newspaper. Complements Bangkok Post.

name: Frontier Myanmar
credibility_notes: Independent Myanmar news. Post-coup coverage. Complements Irrawaddy and Myanmar Now.

name: Americas Quarterly
credibility_notes: Council of the Americas. Latin American politics, economics, policy analysis.

name: The Star Malaysia
credibility_notes: Malaysia's largest English-language newspaper. Star Media Group.

name: The Santiago Times
credibility_notes: Chile's English-language news outlet. Fills Chile coverage gap.

name: Nairobi News
credibility_notes: Nairobi-focused news from Nation Media Group. Complements The East African.

name: Sahara Reporters
credibility_notes: Nigerian investigative digital outlet. Founded 2006. Strong on accountability journalism.

name: The Continent
credibility_notes: Pan-African weekly newsletter/newspaper. Founded 2020. Distributed via WhatsApp across Africa.

name: The Local Sweden
credibility_notes: English-language news covering Sweden. EU member, NATO member since 2024. Essential for Europe edition.

name: The Local Italy
credibility_notes: English-language news covering Italy. G7 economy, EU co-founder. Essential for Europe edition.

name: The Local Denmark
credibility_notes: English-language news covering Denmark. EU member, NATO member. Essential for Europe edition.

name: The Local Norway
credibility_notes: English-language news covering Norway. NATO member, major energy exporter. Essential for Europe edition.

name: The Local Austria
credibility_notes: English-language news covering Austria. EU member, hosts IAEA/OPEC/OSCE headquarters.

name: The Portugal News
credibility_notes: Portugal's leading English-language newspaper. Founded 1988. EU member.

name: Iceland Review
credibility_notes: Iceland's English-language magazine. Founded 1963. NATO member, Arctic affairs.

name: The Local Switzerland
credibility_notes: English-language news covering Switzerland. Hosts UN, WTO, Red Cross, WHO.

name: Kuwait Times
credibility_notes: Kuwait's oldest English-language daily, founded 1961. Independent ownership.

name: Oman Observer
credibility_notes: Oman's English-language daily. State-affiliated.

name: Havana Times
credibility_notes: Independent English-language outlet covering Cuba. Founded 2009. Fills Cuba gap.

name: Guatemala Times
credibility_notes: Guatemala's English-language news outlet.

name: Daily Trust
credibility_notes: Northern Nigeria's leading newspaper. Provides counterweight to Lagos-based outlets. Essential for understanding Nigeria's Muslim north.

name: Vientiane Times
credibility_notes: Laos's only English-language newspaper. State-affiliated.

name: Stabroek News
credibility_notes: Guyana's leading independent newspaper. Essential: Guyana is now the world's fastest-growing economy (oil discovery). Founded 1986.
